TextENGNew: If Hinamizawa could be called a remote hamlet, then the Takatsudo district deserved to be called an abandoned village.
changeReason: Old translates 廃村 as 'desolate'; new correctly translates as 'abandoned village,' matching the specific Japanese term for a village whose population has left.

TextENGNew: As a typical city dweller, I couldn't begin to understand why anyone would have tried to live in a place like this.
Traces of former habitation lingered here and there, but everything was coated in dust and strangled by ivy -- clearly declaring that this was no longer a place where people lived.
``...People actually lived here once.``

"Well, that was a long time ago.@k@vS05/11/100700470. A remote place like this is rough on the elderly.@k@vS05/11/100700471. And the young folks probably want to live somewhere more civilized.@k@vS05/11/100700472. If nobody's left to carry things on, well, this is what happens."
Even without any personal attachment to this place, the sight of its decay stirred a faint melancholy.
Ooishi seemed to feel the same way.
``...The wallet was found here?@k@vS05/13/101300471. The person who found it must have had some reason to be passing through such a remote area.``
``Yeah, some of the villagers have fields up in the mountains.@k@vS05/11/100700474. Those folks pass through here mornings and evenings.``
``The villagers who work the fields walk through here every morning and evening?``
changeReason: Old says 'walk by here every day'; new says 'every morning and evening,' matching 朝晩 (morning and evening) in the Japanese.

TextENGNew: ``No way.@k@vS05/11/100700476. They drive as far as the road allows.``
``And driving through here at dawn or dusk, they just happened to spot a single dropped wallet?``
``...Apparently so.@k@vS05/11/100700478. Na ha ha ha!``
``...Where exactly did the person say they found it?``
``In a thicket a bit further ahead.@k@vS05/11/100700480. The finder apparently had a sudden stomachache and ducked into the bushes to relieve himself.``
``And that's how he happened to find it.@k@vS05/11/100700482. Maybe he was hoping there'd be some tissue paper inside.@k@vS05/11/100700483. Na ha ha ha!``
``...The whole story feels a little too convenient.@k@vS05/13/101300476. From the moment this wallet turned up, I haven't been able to shake that feeling.``
I gave voice to the unease I had felt since the start of the day.
Too convenient -- yet undeniable evidence.
But too perfect... and somehow unsettling.@kThat was the feeling.
``You might actually be onto something with 'too convenient.'``
``...What do you mean?``
``Truth is, I heard something from a friend in the Fourth Division.@k@vS05/11/100700486. Oryou Sonozaki apparently made reference to the minister's grandson's kidnapping.``
``She referenced it?``
``'The poor minister's grandson has been held for so many days now.@k@vS05/11/100700488. It would be nice if he were released soon.' Something like that.``
``Apparently, old Oryou muttered that while sitting on her veranda.@k@vS05/11/100700490. And that little mutter traveled through the Sonozaki family's network of networks to the right ears.``
That's right -- Sato-san, the informant, had told me about this before.
When the matriarch Oryou expressed 'concern,' someone in the family would 'take the initiative.'
``So... that was effectively an order to release the hostage?``
``That's how I see it.@k@vS05/11/100700492. This conveniently discovered wallet was probably staged to hand the grandson over to the police.``
``...Why would they release the hostage?``
``Na ha ha ha!@k@vS05/11/100700494. Well, there's only one reason you'd release a hostage, isn't there?``
``What is it?``
``The deal was struck.@k@vS05/11/100700496. The minister must have promised to cancel the Hinamizawa Dam Project.``
``...The dam project will definitely go away.``
At that moment... the girl's words resurfaced in my mind.
...In the end, everything had played out exactly as she had foretold.
That young girl had known how this would end.
She had known about this kidnapping -- perhaps from beginning to end.
``...Akasaka.``
The girl called my name without warning.
``...What is it?``
``......Go back to Tokyo.``
What...?
``...Before long, you will come to bitterly regret ever setting foot in this village.``
``Because the sight of you then would be so wretched and pitiful.@k@vS05/27/100500111. ...That's why I'm warning you now, while there's still time.``
``...Why... would I come to regret it...?``
``...You ask too many questions.``

``...When you were a child standing in the middle of a crosswalk with the light against you,@k@vS05/27/100500114. did your parents wait until they finished explaining why it was dangerous before pulling you back?``

``They would pull you back first, wouldn't they?@k@vS05/27/100500116. They'd drag you to the sidewalk first, and only then explain why it was dangerous, right?@k@vS05/27/100500117. ...That's what this is.``
...The girl who knew everything from start to finish... the very first warning she gave me. Go back to Tokyo.
``...Akasaka, you scaredy-cat.@k@vS05/27/100500119. ...Hee hee hee hee hee...!``
``The grandson will probably be found safe, but... the aftermath is going to be messy.@k@vS05/11/100700498. How messy, I leave to your imagination.``
``Furude Rika -- a girl by that name.@k@vS05/13/101300486. ...Do you know her?``
What I said was apparently so out of the blue that Ooishi sat in stunned silence for a moment.
``...Of course I know her.@k@vS05/11/100700500. She's the only daughter of the Furude family, one of the Three Families.``
``What kind of girl is she?``
``...Well, well.@k@vS05/11/100700502. That's an unexpected name to bring up. I'm a bit surprised.@k@vS05/11/100700503. What's your interest in that little lady?``
``Oh... no, it's nothing like that.@k@vS05/13/101300489. ...It's just... something that's been on my mind.``
``...Hmm?@k@vS05/11/100700506. Na ha ha ha, well, whatever.@k@vS05/11/100700507. We shared a mahjong table, so I won't pry. That's what friends are for.``
``...I'm honored you'd call me a friend.``
``Nfu fu!@k@vS05/11/100700509. So, that little lady is, well, something like a village mascot.@k@vS05/11/100700510. The villagers practically worship her.``
``Worship?``
``Hmm, I don't know much about it myself.@k@vS05/11/100700513. But apparently, a girl born into the Furude family carries some kind of sacred significance.``
``It's a folk belief in Hinamizawa.@k@vS05/11/100700515. Something about being the reincarnation of Oyashiro-sama, or so the story goes.``
``Oyashiro-sama...?@k@vS05/13/101300493. ...Now that you mention it, I think I saw that name on one of the Defense Alliance's banners.``
``Ah, that's the name of Hinamizawa's guardian deity.@k@vS05/11/100700517. They believe it strikes down anyone who wrongs the village.``
``Well, if such a convenient god existed, the dam workers would have been struck down ages ago.@k@vS05/11/100700519. Fortunately, they seem to be in good health today. Na ha ha ha!``
``The grandson of the minister who wronged the village, struck down by Oyashiro-sama's wrath... what was the term again?@k@vS05/13/101300496. Right -- 'spirited away by the demon.'``
``Na ha ha ha ha!@k@vS05/11/100700521. I see.@k@vS05/11/100700522. That's probably exactly how it would be framed, isn't it.``
``Divine punishment by Oyashiro-sama.@k@vS05/13/101300500. Then... the culprit is the reincarnation of Oyashiro-sama... that girl...?``
``Na ha ha ha ha ha!``
I wasn't even sure what I was saying at that point, so having Ooishi laugh it off was actually a relief.
Ooishi was laughing so heartily that I couldn't help but join in with some idiotic laughter of my own.
``...Oh.@k@vS05/11/100700525. Now that's unusual.``
Ooishi laid on the horn several times, rolled down the window, leaned out, and waved broadly.
An oncoming car.
Until now, we hadn't encountered a single other vehicle.
And of all places, here -- in this desolate wasteland.
Ooishi seemed to recognize the other driver.
The other car gave a brief honk in reply and pulled to a stop alongside us.
``Dr. Irie~~~~~!!!@k@vS05/11/100700527. Good afternoon.``
When the other car's window rolled down, it revealed a young man in a white medical coat.
Close to my age, or perhaps a little older.@k...Though you can never really tell with people.
``Why, if it isn't Ooishi-san.@k@vS05/10/101000016. Good afternoon.@k@vS05/10/101000017. ...Ha ha, what a surprise to run into you here.``
``That's my line, Doctor.@k@vS05/11/100700529. Never thought I'd see you in a place like this. Nfu fu fu!@k@vS05/11/100700530. What brings you all the way out here?``
``Oh, nothing -- just a house call.``
``...Is that so?@k@vS05/11/100700532. An emergency case?``
``No... nothing that serious.@k@vS05/10/101000020. Thankfully.``
``Not needing medical attention is always the best outcome.@k@vS05/11/100700534. Nfu fu fu...!``
``I'll take my leave, then.@k@vS05/10/101000022. ...If I don't hurry back, my staff will have my head.``
``Na ha ha!@k@vS05/11/100700536. Being the head of a clinic must keep you busy.@k@vS05/11/100700537. Well then, take care.``
The young doctor named Irie gave a farewell gesture, then drove off and vanished around the bend in no time.
Ooishi also started the car, but stopped again almost immediately.
He pulled a worn atlas from the glove compartment.
His expression had lost every trace of levity -- he was utterly serious.
``...What is it?``
``The clinic shouldn't be making house calls today.@k@vS05/11/100700539. This would have to be a genuine emergency or a patient with very special circumstances.``
Ooishi flipped through pages until he found the Takatsudo district, then looked up to survey the surrounding landscape and determine our location.
``...You're saying that emergency is suspicious?``
``Didn't I say?@k@vS05/11/100700541. There's practically nobody living in the Takatsudo area.@k@vS05/11/100700542. So who out here is sick?``
Without any familiarity with the area, I couldn't quite grasp what the map was telling me.
``We're heading for where the wallet was found.@k@vS05/11/100700545. The discovery site is here.@k@vS05/11/100700546. And the doctor came from... this direction.``
``...Meaning Dr. Irie came from a direction where there are no residents?``

``Unless someone collapsed while farming on a terraced field up in the mountains.@k@vS05/11/100700549. But if someone collapsed and a doctor was called, you'd normally bring the patient back to the clinic.``

``But just now, Dr. Irie said it was nothing serious.@k@vS05/11/100700551. And there didn't seem to be anyone else in his car.@k@vS05/11/100700552. In other words, the patient is still wherever he was.``
Ooishi said nothing more.
Neither did I.
A doctor had just examined a patient.@kNearby.@kThat was all.
The noisy radio had gone silent at some point.
The sky, too, had turned an oppressive gray without my noticing.
...When Ooishi muttered that it might rain, the sky obliged with a torrential downpour.
Eventually, Ooishi stopped the car.
The only sounds were the drumming of rain on the roof and the mournful squeak of the wipers.
``...Let's do this quietly.``
Speaking in a low voice, he stepped out of the car without an umbrella, careful not to make a sound.
Ooishi crouched down and pointed through the underbrush.
On the other side was a prefabricated shed, and parked beside it, a car.
The car was clearly well-maintained -- not a vehicle that had been abandoned for years.
``...What's that shed?``
``A forestry service equipment shed.@k@vS05/11/100700554. I'm told it's not used outside of summer.``
``Does that car belong to someone from the forestry service?``
``Who knows.@k@vS05/11/100700556. Doesn't look like a government vehicle to me.``
Ooishi maintained his composure, but my nerves were ratcheting tighter by the second.
``Just as a precaution.``
With those words, Ooishi returned to the car.
I thought he was going back for an umbrella, but what he grabbed was the car's radio.
``Hello, this is Ooishi.@k@vS05/11/100700559. Do you read?@k@vS05/11/100700560. Greetings, greetings.``
``Yes, Okinomiya PS here.@k@vS05/00/VTP_OkeijiA_0002. Ooishi-san, reading you loud and clear, over.``
``I'm currently in front of the forestry service equipment shed in the Takatsudo district.@k@vS05/11/100700562. Not the Yagauchi side -- the one you reach through Hinamizawa.``
``...Affirmative, confirmed.``
``Suspicious persons in the shed.@k@vS05/11/100700564. Proceeding to investigate.@k@vS05/11/100700565. If you don't hear from me in five minutes, send backup.``
``Roger that.``
``Well then... shall we go have a look?@k@vS05/11/100700568. Nfu fu fu!``
Since being confined here, he had heard the sound of cars several times.
And so he'd assumed the distant car he heard now was just another one of those.
But his captors' reaction was unlike anything he had seen before.
They flinched as though jolted by electricity, then pressed themselves against the window and peered outside.
``...They stopped.``
``...Someone from the forestry service?@k@vS05/49/VT2_13hibari1053. This is bad.``
``...No... looks like police.``
One of the captors seized the boy by the collar and pressed a blade against his cheek.
``Stay quiet for a bit, kid.@k@vS05/49/VT2_13hibari1055. I think you know this, but if you start yelling, things are going to get ugly.``
Toshiki Inukai was certain that the doctor had reported them to the police.
He felt a surge of relief knowing rescue was near, but the thought of what his cornered captors might do kept him from relaxing.
``What do we do?``
``...Buy us time.@k@vS05/14/104700043. Take the kid and slip out the back.@k@vS05/14/104700044. I'll handle this.``
One of the captors grabbed the boy by the collar and hauled him to his feet.
Toshiki Inukai tried to resist by intensifying his feigned illness, but his captors paid him no heed.
BANG BANG BANG!!!
Someone was pounding violently on the door.
BANG BANG BANG, BANG!!
Another round of pounding.
Toshiki Inukai hesitated for a split second -- should he call out in response?
But before he could decide, his mouth was covered, and that choice was taken from him.
The leader among the captors signaled 'go' with a wave of his hand.
The younger captor nodded, and with the boy's mouth still muffled, headed for the back exit.
``Yeah, yeah~~ need somethin'?``
``Hello there.@k@vS05/11/100700572. This is the police. Would you mind opening up?@k@vS05/11/100700573. Just a routine check.``
``Sure, sure.@k@vS05/14/104700047. Just a sec, lemme ope--``
The instant the lock was undone, Ooishi shoved the door wide open.
With an intimidating glare, he pushed the man aside and stepped into the room.
He swept the cramped interior in a single glance -- empty. The grandson he was looking for was nowhere to be seen.
``Hmm.@k@vS05/11/100700576. I was told this was a forestry equipment shed.@k@vS05/11/100700577. Doesn't look like equipment storage to me.``
There was nothing inside that resembled an equipment shed.
Only a heap of blankets suggesting someone had been sleeping here, and scattered bags of food waste.
``...Aah, the place's been empty for ages.@k@vS05/14/104700049. Never seen any equipment stored 'ere.``
``But this is the forestry service's building, isn't it?@k@vS05/11/100700579. Are you from the forestry service?``
``...'Ow 'bout you? Police?``
The two men glared at each other with mutual suspicion.
...Neither Ooishi nor the man was willing to identify himself first.
The man, sensing from the sharpness of Ooishi's gaze that talking his way out would be difficult.
No matter what excuse he fabricated, a few dozen seconds of stalling was the best he could manage.
Only the sound of rain filled this taut silence.
Then, from somewhere in the distance, came the sounds of a scuffle -- the unmistakable noise of a physical struggle.
Ooishi's instincts fired.
Akasaka, whom he had sent around back, must have run into a captor trying to flee with the hostage...!
But the man in front of him reacted a hair faster than Ooishi did.
His strike was less a punch to the face and more a calculated attempt to blind Ooishi's vision.
In the instant Ooishi's guard dropped, the man drove a kick into his groin without hesitation.
...But it didn't land cleanly, and failed to be a decisive blow.
The man grabbed Ooishi by the throat with both hands and tried to bulldoze him to the ground.
But as Ooishi toppled backward, he planted his foot in the man's gut as if launching a counter-kick.
Using that leg as a fulcrum, the man was hurled over the falling Ooishi in something resembling a tomoe throw.
The two faced off in the driving rain.
``Well, isn't this interesting.@k@vS05/11/100700581. You wanna go?!@k@vS05/11/100700582. Nahahaha!``
As if to psych himself up, Ooishi flashed a fearless grin.
He brushed off the mud and rose to his feet.
Despite being thrown, the man recovered with far more grace and speed than Ooishi's ungainly scramble upright.
``Ooishi-saaaaan!!@k@vS05/13/101300508. Hurry!!``
Akasaka's voice rang out from the distance.
From its urgency, it was clear his situation was equally critical, and he needed Ooishi there as soon as possible.
But Ooishi's hands were full right here.
``Akasaka-saaan!@k@vS05/11/100700584. Sorry, but hang on just a minute!``
The man raised both fists in a stance that unmistakably telegraphed training in karate or boxing.
Ooishi, naturally, had the standard martial arts training expected of a police officer.
And the grit earned from surviving plenty of tight spots.
But -- no, precisely because of that -- he could tell the man in front of him was several levels above him in a fight.
...Dammit! I should never have assumed this would go smoothly just because Oryou had given the order to release the hostage!
``Not bad at all.@k@vS05/11/100700586. It's been a while since I've felt this queasy.@k@vS05/11/100700587. Na ha ha ha!``
Ooishi projected an air of composure.
His opponent, reading that, seemed to conclude that Ooishi wouldn't go down easily.
The man charged, lunging to grab hold of Ooishi!
If grappled from that low stance, Ooishi would be tackled to the ground and mounted.
It looked like a schoolyard brawl, but in reality it was a near-inescapable hold.
Ooishi met the charge head-on, dropping his own center of gravity to brace for the collision.
The instant they clashed, Ooishi seized the man's lapels!
And as he wrenched them upward, he drove his elbow into the man's solar plexus with all his might.@kIt was Ooishi's signature move -- street-fighting judo.
He grabbed on with his other hand, attempting to hurl his opponent with both arms!
But the man shifted his weight and swept his arms in a wide arc, entangling both of Ooishi's.
Worse still, both of Ooishi's elbows were wrenched outward, leaving him in an unnatural position with his back nearly exposed and both arms pinned against the man's chest!
This guy... he knew grappling or aikido!
Ooishi, realizing his fingers were getting twisted and caught in the man's shirt, chose to release his grip voluntarily.
But he couldn't recover from his compromised stance.@kNot at this distance!
The man read Ooishi's attempt to rotate out of it perfectly, slipping around to his blind side.
And then, with both palms -- *CRACK!!*@kHe clapped them hard over Ooishi's ears.
A double ear slap was a forbidden technique in virtually every martial art except pure self-defense.
Neither karate nor judo permitted it.
It was that simple -- and that dangerous!
Ooishi's hands shot up reflexively to clutch his ears.
But before they could reach them, the man had already locked his arm around Ooishi's neck.
A thick bicep clamped around the base of his neck like a steel bolt.
Ooishi instinctively thought: he's going to kill me.
A man this skilled in close combat, with this grip from behind -- snapping a neck would be trivial.
But his opponent chose to squeeze and choke rather than break.
And so, even though Ooishi's face was contorted, he managed a grin.
His opponent had the chance to kill him and chose not to.
He thought to himself:@k'So he doesn't intend to kill me.'
But that didn't mean the chokehold was gentle.
His consciousness began to dim with alarming speed as his vision flooded from black to a blood-red haze.
Having been choked out several times in judo during his student days, Ooishi recognized the feeling and accepted it -- this was locked in tight.
Akasaka had just barely recovered from the searing pain of the groin kick.
He had run straight into the captor carrying the boy at the back exit.
Though he knew Toshiki Inukai's face well from photographs, he couldn't immediately identify the tape-gagged boy before him as the real person.
And it was hardly surprising that someone with his limited experience would freeze up, unsure how to handle a situation like this.
His captor, naturally, didn't let that momentary opening go to waste.
The man's groin kick landed squarely, and Akasaka didn't even have time to call out to Ooishi.
The man slung the boy over his shoulder and tried to circle around to the front of the shed.
But hearing Ooishi's booming voice from that direction, he abandoned the idea.
Instead, he bolted straight for the forest.
As though the boy on his shoulder weighed nothing at all.
Akasaka hesitated for a split second.
Linking up with Ooishi, who was fighting the enemy, would be the safer choice.
But he couldn't afford to lose sight of the fleeing man now!
Akasaka scrambled to his feet and gave chase.
He crashed through low brush, snapping twigs and crunching through fallen leaves.
Branches scraped and clawed at him, leaving a growing collection of fresh cuts.
He kept splashing through puddle after puddle, and in no time his shoes were waterlogged with mud.
For Akasaka, who had never run through a forest like this, it was profoundly unpleasant even under these urgent circumstances.
The moment the words left his mouth, he regretted wasting precious oxygen on them.
What thief in the world would politely stop when told to by the police?!
For a city boy like Akasaka, barreling through this forest was grueling,@kbut it was clearly no easier for a man running with a boy slung over his shoulder.
He wasn't gaining ground, but he wasn't losing any either.
Once Akasaka realized that, he recovered a sliver of composure.
Just keep chasing.
The conditions were the same.@kIf anything, the man running in that unstable position was at a far greater disadvantage.
He couldn't run forever.@kSooner or later, he would definitely stumble!
As long as Akasaka kept the pressure on, the man would trip -- it was inevitable!!
And that wish was granted.
The man stepped on something, lost his balance, and -- unable to bear the boy's weight any longer -- dropped him.
Akasaka, in all honesty, didn't care about the captor.
All that mattered was securing Toshiki Inukai's safety.
If the captor ran off alone, that was perfectly fine.
...But the boy was evidently too valuable to abandon, and the man didn't choose to flee.
Akasaka didn't know what to do in a situation like this.
The top priority was protecting the grandson -- subduing the captor was secondary.
He hadn't planned for a physical confrontation.
But it was probably do or die!!
That split-second of hesitation proved fatal.
The man threw a quick jab aimed between -- or rather, directly at -- both of Akasaka's eyes.
When Akasaka raised both hands to shield his face, the man drove a devastating kick into his unprotected stomach.
The pain felt like his organs were being wrenched out of him, but Akasaka didn't flinch -- he lunged forward and wrapped his arms around the man's leg.
But the man didn't hesitate.
Leading with his trapped leg, he threw his full body weight onto Akasaka, tackling him to the ground.
Akasaka was knocked down, but he absolutely refused to release his grip on the leg.
The two men collapsed in a tangled heap.
The man struggled desperately to wrench his leg free, but having fallen in an awkward position, he couldn't get the leverage.
They rolled across the ground, each scrambling for a superior position.
But Akasaka, with both hands locked around his opponent's knee, was at a clear disadvantage against a man with his upper body free.
Once the man gained positional advantage, he hammered Akasaka's head with both fists as hard as he could.
And when he found a rock nearby, he started bashing Akasaka's skull with that!
CRACK!@|@y CRACK!!@|@y CRACK!!!
The difference between being punched and being hit with a rock was beyond comparison!!
Every instinct screamed at him to release the man's leg and protect his head.
But... if he let go now, the man would escape!
If he didn't hold on here, everything would be for nothing.
The grandson he had come to save was right there.
He was going to save this boy and go back to Tokyo!!
And then he'd be done with this godforsaken village...!
BAM!!@|@y CRACK!!@|@y THUD!!!
Without looking, I could tell my forehead had split open and was gushing blood.
But I didn't let go of his leg.@kI wouldn't. I refused.
When the man realized no amount of beating would pry me loose, he went for my throat -- both hands crushing my Adam's apple.
I pulled my chin down tight in a feeble attempt to resist, but it was useless.
His hands engulfed my throat, squeezing my windpipe with vise-like force!!
It was less the agony of suffocation and more a choking, retching torment.
My stomach felt like it was turning inside out!!@kBut I could only endure it...!
...Dammit, dammit, dammit!!!
My throat... ngh... hurts...!!
I won't let go. I won't let go...!!
I don't want to be here.
I want this to be over. I want to go back to Tokyo!
Go back to Tokyo and talk with Yukie about our baby, plan our future together... let our hopes bloom into something beautiful...!!
I hadn't consciously loosened my grip.@k...But the slightest slackening freed the man's leg.
The man kicked out with everything he had, and I tumbled away.
My head was spinning. I couldn't get up.@k...No -- my entire body had no strength left.
Don't give up...!!!
If I let him escape now... a chance like this will never come again!!
Even catching their trail had been a miracle built on miracles.
If we couldn't recover the grandson here... there would be no second miracle!!
The man was trying to hoist the boy again, but the boy put up unexpected resistance, and the man failed to notice me getting back up.
I grabbed the rock he had used to beat my head.@k...Now it's my turn!!
``YAAAAARRRRGH!!!``
If the rock could compensate for my lack of strength... I could land a solid blow!!
My rock-clenched fist drove into the man's soft flank.@kIt was a devastating hit.
The man rolled on the ground, clutching his side.
Freed from his grip, the boy quickly darted behind me.
``You're Toshiki Inukai, aren't you?!@k@vS05/13/101300513. I've come to rescue you!!``
To the boy, there could have been no words he wanted to hear more...!
But the crisis was far from over.
Because even while clutching his side, the man had risen to his feet again and reassumed his fighting stance.
In his eyes I could see both the hatred born of pain and the confidence that he could beat me.
If we clashed head-on again, a split forehead might be the least of my worries.
``Stop resisting!!@k@vS05/13/101300515. Surrender quietly!!``
``You're the one who should hand the kid over!!@k@vS05/49/VT2_13hibari1060. You want your neck snapped?!``
``Who would yield to the likes of you?!@k@vS05/13/101300517. Backup is on its way!@k@vS05/13/101300518. Give it up!!``
The man reacted sharply to the word 'backup.'
He realized that standing here arguing wasn't just wasting time -- it was putting him in increasing danger.
The speed with which he shifted gears was almost frightening.
``Then let's make this quick!!@k@vS05/49/VT2_13hibari1062. How about this...!!``
What the man pulled from his back pocket was -- of all things -- a handgun!
Of course, I was a police officer too.
It wasn't as if I didn't know what a gun was.
But having one pointed at me was something I had never experienced.
Was it... real?
Of course it was real!!@kWho would pull out a toy gun in a situation like this?!
``Put your hands behind your head and lie face down -- now!!``
``Don't get carried away, criminal!!@k@vS05/13/101300521. I'm not obeying you!!``
``You goddamn idiot!!@k@vS05/49/VT2_13hibari1065. Do I have to put a bullet in you before you understand what this is?!``
``Then go ahead and shoot!@k@vS05/13/101300523. The sound of your gunshot will bring backup running!!``
``You moron!@k@vS05/49/VT2_13hibari1067. We're deep in a forest, in this rain!!@k@vS05/49/VT2_13hibari1068. Nobody's hearing a damn thing!!``
As long as he had the gun, no amount of bluster on my part would change the overwhelming imbalance.
And yet, I sensed an opening.
If our positions were reversed, I wouldn't waste time on a shouting match -- I'd shoot, take the boy, and run.
That would be faster, and the gunshot might even cow the boy into compliance.
But the man wasn't shooting. He was demanding my surrender.
Which meant he either couldn't shoot or didn't want to.@k...In other words, this man was soft!
Every second I bought tipped the scales further in my favor.
Ooishi would come. And the backup we'd called for before arriving should already be en route.
And that wasn't all -- the police box should have been contacted too.
A village-stationed officer could reach us far sooner than anyone else!
``...Dammit...!!@k@vS05/49/VT2_13hibari1070. Do I have to shoot you before you get it through your skull?!``
``There's no escape!!@k@vS05/13/101300525. Don't make things worse for yourself!!``
Then I heard it -- heavy footsteps crashing through the brush, drawing closer.
If Ooishi reached us, everything would change!
Against a man like him, one gun would mean nothing...!
``Ooishi-san!!@k@vS05/13/101300527. Over here! Over here!!!@k@vS05/13/101300528. ...What?!``
I had believed time was on my side.
But I had failed to consider... that the enemy, too, could have reinforcements...!!
One look at the man who appeared was enough to identify him as another captor.
``Whaddya doin'...@k@vS05/14/104700052. Hurry it up, the cops are comin'!@k@vS05/14/104700053. What's takin' so long with one guy?!``
``...Dammit...!!@k@vS05/13/101300530. What happened to Ooishi-san?!``
``That barrel of a man?@k@vS05/14/104700055. Put 'im down. Foamin' at the mouth.@k@vS05/14/104700056. Heh heh heh.``
The boy, sensing the danger acutely, pressed himself against my back.
...How could this be... dammit...!!
``Your buddy ain't comin'!!@k@vS05/49/VT2_13hibari1072. Serves you right!!@k@vS05/49/VT2_13hibari1073. Now do as you're told!!``
As I backed against a thick tree, shielding the boy behind me, the second man also drew a handgun.
``I'll say this once more.@k@vS05/14/104700058. Hands on yer head. Face down. Now.``
``I refuse!!!``
The instant I shouted my defiance, blood erupted from my left shoulder. A fraction of a second later, I realized I had been shot!
The first real gunshot I ever heard was far more mundane and anticlimactic than any gunshot in a movie or TV drama.
If anything, it sounded like the little firecrackers I used to buy at the candy shop as a kid.
But the pain was beyond anything I could compare...@kThe terror of a gun -- you only truly understood it after being shot.
``...S-Sir?!@k@vS05/00/toshi00014. Are you okay?!@k@vS05/00/toshi00015. Are you okay?!``
The panicking boy tugged at my clothes, aggravating the wound and sending bolts of pain through me.
I wanted to put on a brave face and reassure him, but my voice came out hoarse -- more alarming than comforting.
``I-I'm fine!!@k@vS05/13/101300536. Backup has already been called.@k@vS05/13/101300537. They'll be here any moment.``
``Be quicker to put one through yer skull 'fore the cops get 'ere.@k@vS05/14/104700060. The main house told us no killin', but this is self-defense now.``
...This was bad.@k...This was very, very bad.
This second man was a completely different breed from the first!
No hesitation. No mercy!!
If he said he would shoot, he would shoot!
He wasn't someone a man like me could buy time against!!
At that moment -- and I hated myself for it -- Yukie's smiling face flashed through my mind.
I knew what that meant.
I was warning myself not to throw my life away over stubborn pride.
Of course I didn't want to die here!
My new life with Yukie had only just begun, and I wanted to see the face of our child who would be born any day now!!
Was I really going to stake my life on the minister's grandson when I was this close to becoming a father myself?!!
...Arrgh, dammit!!
Stop spiraling!@k Then what do I do?!@k Hand over the boy and save my own skin?!@k Brilliant plan!!@k Everyone values their own life!!
Ahh, damn... it hurts... it hurts...!!!
If I'd known it would be this painful, I never would have played the tough guy...!!
And... the biggest miscalculation of all was that the gunshot was far quieter than I'd imagined.
A sound that feeble would be swallowed by the rain and the forest!
It wouldn't carry far enough for anyone to hear...!!!
``Dammit!!@k@vS05/13/101300540. Wait -- just wait!!@k@vS05/13/101300541. I don't want to die! Not here, not like this!``
``And I don't wanna kill ya either!!@k@vS05/14/104700063. Just do as I say and we're good!!``
``Don't shoot, okay?!@k@vS05/13/101300543. Don't shoot!!@k@vS05/13/101300544. I have a wife waiting for me at home! She's about to give birth!!``
``I won't shoot 'slong as ya don't yell!@k@vS05/14/104700065. Lie face down with yer hands on yer head, and I won't shoot!!``
``Promise?!@k@vS05/13/101300547. You promise?!@k@vS05/13/101300548. Keep your word, you hear me?!``
``I will!!@k@vS05/14/104700067. A man's word between men!!``
``Keep it! You better keep it!!!@k@vS05/13/101300551. All right, I'm getting down.@k@vS05/13/101300552. I'm getting down, so don't shoot, okay?!``
``You're still going to shoot me, aren't you?!@k@vS05/13/101300555. I know you're thinking it!@k@vS05/13/101300556. The moment I'm on the ground, you'll --``
``I won't, I won't!!@k@vS05/14/104700069. Fine!!@k@vS05/14/104700070. I'll put it away!@k@vS05/14/104700071. See? It's down, it's lowered. Satisfied?``
``Yeah, absolutely no complaints!!!``
It was over in a flash.
The instant the gun was lowered, Ooishi struck -- and he didn't miss his chance!
Ooishi had been watching from behind, waiting for an opening.
But with both men pointing guns, he hadn't had a chance to move.
Reading Ooishi's eye signal begging me to create an opening, I had fulfilled that mission admirably.
Ooishi seized the gun arm from behind and locked the joint in one fluid motion.
Stripping the gun from the man's hand took barely a heartbeat!
The other man, a beat slow in processing what had happened, raised his gun, but couldn't fire at Ooishi while he was grappling with his companion.
If the second man had been the one now free, he might have fired without hesitation.
But this man wouldn't shoot.@kHe couldn't!!
Ooishi kicked the disarmed man away, the stolen gun in his left hand, and drew his own service weapon with his right.
The barrels of his two-gun stance were trained on each man!
``I believe that's checkmate!!``
It seemed like a perfect endgame.
But despite having guns pointed at them, the men shouted 'Run!!' and bolted in opposite directions.
It was over in the blink of an eye.
That those two would flee without resistance or retaliation was genuinely unexpected.
Ooishi clicked his tongue softly and stowed both guns in his pockets.
``Tch.@k@vS05/11/100700592. Those guys know the rules well...``
``An officer can't shoot a fleeing suspect's back.@k@vS05/11/100700594. And I'd hate to lose my pension.``
Ooishi and I shared a grin.
``Are you Toshiki Inukai-kun?@k@vS05/11/100700596. The grandson of the Minister of Construction?``
``...Well, this is going to be a big case.@k@vS05/11/100700598. Though it'll probably end up as a sacrificial tail-cut.@k@vS05/11/100700599. Nfu fu fu fu!``
Ooishi chuckled with evident amusement.
It was obvious that the Onigafuchi Defense Alliance (or the Sonozaki family pulling their strings) was behind this, but knowing it and proving it were very different things.
And, with apologies to Ooishi, whether this case would ever see the light of day was doubtful.
If it became public, the minister's downfall would be inevitable.
Headquarters, prioritizing the national interest, would most likely bury the whole affair quietly.
Put bluntly: born in shadow, returned to shadow.
...A veteran like Ooishi probably understood all of that already.
``For now, let's set up a perimeter.@k@vS05/11/100700601. Those guys still have at least one gun.@k@vS05/11/100700602. We need to be careful.``
As the tension drained away, the pain from the gunshot wound in my shoulder came roaring back.
My forehead throbbed and burned as though it were on fire.
I thought I was drenched in sweat from the dissipating tension, but when I wiped my forehead, it came away not with sweat but thick, copious blood.
My shirt, I realized, was stained crimson from the blood streaming down my face.
I looked back.
...The boy was safe.@kBeyond any doubt.
The investigation into the fleeing suspects would continue, but that was a matter for another day.
For now, I could state with certainty that we had cleared the most critical hurdle: securing the grandson.
The moment I allowed myself to acknowledge that, I felt the lights in my head going out one by one, plunging everything into darkness.
My knees buckled, and the ground felt as soft as a feather bed.
Even as I sank into the rain-soaked mud, I felt no discomfort whatsoever.
Ooishi rushed over, asking if I was all right, but somehow it didn't feel like it was happening to me.@k...I reached for the last remaining switch in my mind, and turned off the light.
A long, slow exhale... and total surrender.@kDrowsiness.@k...Something softer than any blanket wrapped itself around me...
